name: space
label: Space Exploration — Agencies, Missions & Astronauts
description: Space agencies, historic missions, astronauts, spacecraft and planetary science.
observations: NASA is the United States national space agency founded in 1958
NASA is headquartered in Washington D.C.
ESA is the European Space Agency founded by 22 member states
SpaceX is a private aerospace company founded by Elon Musk in 2002
Elon Musk is the CEO of SpaceX
SpaceX is headquartered in Hawthorne California
The International Space Station is a joint project of NASA ESA Roscosmos JAXA and CSA
The ISS orbits Earth at an altitude of approximately 408 kilometers
Neil Armstrong was the first human to walk on the Moon in 1969
Neil Armstrong flew on the Apollo 11 mission as commander
Apollo 11 was launched by NASA on July 16 1969
Peggy Whitson holds the record for most time in space by a NASA astronaut
Peggy Whitson served as commander of the ISS three times
Chris Hadfield is a Canadian astronaut who commanded the ISS in 2013
Chris Hadfield flew to the ISS aboard a Soyuz spacecraft
The Artemis program is NASA's plan to return humans to the Moon
Artemis aims to land the first woman and first person of color on the Moon
SpaceX Starship is the launch vehicle designed for Artemis lunar missions
Voyager 1 is the farthest human-made object from Earth
Voyager 1 was launched by NASA in 1977 and entered interstellar space in 2012
Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun and NASA's primary planetary target
NASA's Perseverance rover landed on Mars in Jezero Crater in February 2021
Perseverance is searching for signs of ancient microbial life on Mars
SpaceX plans to send humans to Mars using the Starship spacecraft
The James Webb Space Telescope was launched in December 2021
James Webb Space Telescope is operated jointly by NASA ESA and CSA
ESA's Rosetta mission landed on comet 67P in 2014
The Hubble Space Telescope has been operated by NASA since 1990
NASA's Artemis program is based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida
SpaceX Falcon 9 is a reusable rocket that has completed over 200 launches
test_queries: Who was the first human to walk on the Moon and which mission did they fly?
What is the Artemis program and who is building its launch vehicle?
Which astronaut has spent the most time in space?
What is Voyager 1 and who launched it?
How are NASA and SpaceX connected to the ISS?
